dt-option.name: searchCols
dt-option.summary: Define an initial search for individual columns.

deprecated.description:
As of DataTables 3, the `-init columns.search` option is preferred over this option. The advantage of `-init columns.search` is that the search is more tightly coupled to the column and in combination with `-init columnDefs` you do not need to specify a value for every column.
This option is still supported, but you are encouraged to use `-init columns.search`.

dt-option.description:
Basically the same as the `-init search` option, but in this case for individual columns, rather than the global filter, this option defined the filtering to apply to the table during initialisation.
The array must be of the same size as the number of columns, and each element be an object with the optional parameters defined by `-type DataTable.SearchOptions`. `null` is also accepted and the default will be used. See the `-init search` documentation for more information on these parameters.

example.title: Set initial filtering on second and fourth columns
